title: LogiXpair — Multi-Tenant Freight Forwarding Platform
summary: A multi-tenant B2B SaaS platform built from scratch to replace an existing enterprise freight forwarding system at PT Logimark Forwarding Indonesia. Covers end-to-end freight operations: shipment and consolidation management, AR/AP billing, electronic documents, HBL/AWB print templates, multi-tenant SSO, and full audit logging.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: PT Logimark Forwarding Indonesia is part of a regional freight forwarding group with operations across Asia. LogiXpair is being built to replace an expensive legacy operations platform while keeping control of system ownership, deployment, and long-term product direction.
The rollout is planned in phases: first for Logimark Indonesia, then wider internal group adoption, then potential commercial SaaS availability for freight forwarding teams.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: SSO System: multi-tenant authentication, user management, encrypted credentials, JWT/JWKS, HTTP-only cookie sessions.
Master Data & References: locations, shipment modes, carrier data, and system-wide reference tables.
Cargoflow: booking, shipment, consolidation (consol), and SyncProc modules.
Registry: shipment and consolidation mode/detail/billing records.
Billing: AR/AP invoices with cost/sell split, postings, and accrual tracking.
E-Docs: electronic document management across 6+ entity types.
Printing: 9 print template variants including HBL, AWB, and Delivery Orders.
User & Tenant Admin: full tenant lifecycle and user permission management.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Unified TypeScript ecosystem across all 4 repositories (BE SSO, BE APP, FE SSO, FE APP) — Bun + Express + tRPC + Prisma + Better Auth on the backend; Angular 21 + PrimeNG + TanStack Query + Signals (Zoneless) on the frontend.

Multi-tenant architecture: 1 shared authentication database with isolated per-tenant application databases and tenant connection pooling (1 cached Prisma client per tenant, 5-retry SSO fallback, idle disconnect).
tRPC contracts published as private NPM packages for end-to-end type safety across repositories.
Full audit logging on every mutation.
CI/CD pipeline: push → build → Docker deploy → health check; backend compiled to a single Bun executable (zero source code exposure); frontend obfuscated and served via Nginx.
Custom scaffolding tooling that reduced new module development from days to hours.
Inline Excel-like row-editing components for batch data entry without repetitive modal cycles.
Fault-tolerant file upload pipeline: temp directory (1–24hr lifecycle) → metadata validation → permanent storage.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Delivered the platform solo in under 6 months at approximately 3–4x industry average solo developer output
Engineering a system capable of replacing enterprise-grade freight forwarding software with an internally owned platform
Custom scaffolding tooling compresses new module build time from days to hours, sustaining high output velocity throughout active development.

title: PPIC PIL CKW
summary: A PPIC-focused web system for the CKW (Cikawung) region handling stage-1 production — converting raw materials into usable materials — with HRIS integration for workforce and R&D for planning new item types. Outputs transit to CKP (Cikampek) for SO-driven processing.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Built for the PPIC division in CKW to manage stage-1 production: converting raw materials into usable materials that are shipped to CKP (Cikampek) for Sales Order–driven processing.
Includes a workforce (HRIS) facet and R&D support for planning and creating new item types.
Due to confidentiality, only a subset of screenshots are shown.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Master data: items, machines, usage records, on‑going machines.
Machine map/visualization to reflect real‑time utilization.
HRIS: workforce management and assignment to lines/stations.
R&D: plan and trial new item types before operationalization.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Next.js + React + TypeScript for fast UI iteration and SSR where helpful.
TanStack Query for data fetching/caching and optimistic UX.
Ant Design for consistent, accessible UI components.
Prisma for schema modeling and type‑safe data access.
Bull queues for background processing and long‑running jobs.
Multer and Nodemailer for uploads and event notifications.
REST API with Express/Node.js for service boundaries.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Streamlined production coordination, clearer machine utilization, and tighter HRIS/R&D alignment.

title: PMS - Sales Performance Management
summary: A web platform for sales performance management with interactive dashboards and streamlined workflows. Covers PR→PO procurement, customer PO, quotations and pricing, Sales Orders with recommended auto-prices, lead management, and end‑to‑end SO tracking.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Designed to give operations and commercial teams real‑time visibility of sales productivity, pipeline health, and order fulfillment—from lead to invoice.
Consolidates pricing logic, quotations, orders, and procurement (PR→PO) to reduce manual handoffs and improve SLA adherence.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Leads & Marketing: capture sources, qualify leads, and progress opportunities.
Sales Quotation & Quotation Pricing: generate quotes with recommended price baselines; manual adjustments supported with audit trails.
Sales Order (SO): create SOs from approved quotes; automatic price suggestions based on strategy and cost inputs.
SO Tracking: end‑to‑end status, fulfillment progress, and exceptions.
Master Data: items, customers, pricing rules, sales teams/territories.
Procurement: PR→PO workflow for purchasing materials and services.
Customer PO: register and reconcile customer POs against quotations/SOs.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Next.js + React + TypeScript for fast, type‑safe iteration and SSR/ISR where helpful.
TanStack Query for caching, revalidation, and optimistic UX.
Ant Design for consistent, accessible UI with fast CRUD scaffolding.
Prisma for schema modeling and type‑safe DB access; REST API with Express/Node.js.
Bull for background jobs (price recomputations, email notifications, document generation).
Multer for uploads (supporting attachments on PR/PO/Quote); Nodemailer for approval flows.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Centralized sales pipeline and order tracking improved visibility and reduced cross‑team coordination friction.
Automated price recommendations shortened quotation turnaround and standardized pricing practices.
Streamlined PR→PO and customer PO handling reduced manual errors and sped up fulfillment.

title: PPIC PIL CKP
summary: A PPIC system for CKP (Cikampek) handling final production stages: shipment, surat jalan, stock opname, returns, and stock handling. Built to tighten control, traceability, and throughput at the end of the production pipeline.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Focused on the last-mile of production operations in CKP: ensuring goods are shipped with correct documentation (surat jalan), inventories are accurate (stock opname), and discrepancies are resolved swiftly (returns/adjustments).
Complements CKW’s stage‑1 output by providing downstream control and auditability before customer delivery or inter‑plant transfers.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Shipment & Surat Jalan: create, print/export, and track delivery notes with validation against SO/DO.
Stock Opname: plan and execute cycle counts; reconcile variances with approval workflows.
Returns Handling: register return reasons, link to shipments/SOs, and process put‑back or scrapping.
Stock Adjustments: controlled adjustments with maker–checker approvals and audit logs.
Inventory Views: item/lot/warehouse balances with movement history and filters.
Master Data: warehouses/locations, units of measure, reasons/catalogs for returns/adjustments.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Next.js + React + TypeScript for fast, type‑safe UI development and SSR where helpful.
TanStack Query for caching, background revalidation, and optimistic updates in inventory workflows.
Ant Design for consistent, accessible enterprise UI patterns.
Prisma for schema modeling and type‑safe DB access; REST API built on Express/Node.js.
Bull for background jobs (document generation, nightly reconciliations, notification batching).
WebSockets for inventory scaling events.
Multer for attachment uploads (proof of delivery, counting photos); Nodemailer for approvals/alerts.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Improved inventory integrity via structured counting and approvals, reducing mismatches in outbound operations.
Faster shipment preparation with standardized surat jalan workflows and document generation.
Higher traceability of returns and adjustments, enabling quicker root cause analysis.

title: MPIL CKP
summary: A web and mobile-integrated PPIC system for CKP (Cikampek) focused on final-stage execution: shipment (scan during loading/unloading) and stock opname (scan for inventory audits). Built with handheld Sunmi scanners and robust sync to ensure real-time accuracy on the floor.

sections.title: Context
paragraphs: Targets the last-mile of PPIC execution: scanning stock on loading/unloading for shipments and performing stock opname via handheld devices. Ensures high data fidelity at the point of activity.
Complements upstream production systems by enforcing real-time control, auditability, and reconciliation before dispatch or inventory close.

sections.title: Scope & Modules
bullets: Mobile Loading/Unloading Scans: barcode/QR scanning of lots/items with validation against SO/DO and live exceptions.
Stock Opname Sessions: guided counting with variance flags, partial saves, and approvals.
Offline-first Capture: queue scans while offline; auto-sync when connectivity is restored.
Device & User Management: register devices (Sunmi), assign roles, and track scan activity per operator.
Label/Document Support: print or attach proof (e.g., loading photos) and link to shipment records.
Inventory Views: live balances and movements reflecting mobile scan updates.

sections.title: Technical Decisions
bullets: Ionic + Capacitor for cross-platform mobile with native plugins (Sunmi Scanner SDK) while sharing UI paradigms with the web app.
WebSockets for real-time item scaling.
TanStack Query for resilient caching/sync and optimistic UI on both web and mobile.
Prisma with REST (Express/Node.js) for type-safe persistence and clean service boundaries.
Bull queues for background sync, document generation, and reconciliation jobs.
Multer for attachment uploads (proof-of-loading), Nodemailer for approvals/notifications.
RBAC and audit logs on scan/edit operations for traceability and compliance.

sections.title: Outcomes
paragraphs: Reduced loading/unloading discrepancies through enforced scans and real-time validation.
Higher stock integrity and faster variance resolution during stock opname.
Improved operational visibility with device-level activity tracking and live dashboards.
